sourceMovie.title: Kung Fu Panda 2
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Kung Fu Panda 2 works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Big Hero 6
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kung Fu Panda 2 clicked for you, Big Hero 6 (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Lego Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Kung Fu Panda 2 clicked for you, The Lego Movie (2014) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and family, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Kung Fu Panda 2?
faq.answer: Try Big Hero 6, The Lego Movie, Kung Fu Panda 3—each shares DNA with Kung Fu Panda 2 while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Kung Fu Panda 2?
faq.answer: Kung Fu Panda 2 (2011) is best described as Animation, Family, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Kung Fu Panda 2 part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Kung Fu Panda Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
